message:
chore: close epics ft-3681t.3 (swarm runtime) and ft-3681t.7 (observability)

ft-3681t.3: All 7 children complete — fleet launcher, scheduler,
work queue, MCP coordination, pipeline runtime, lock orchestration,
and simulation/regression suite.

ft-3681t.7: All 6 children complete — unified telemetry, dashboards,
capacity governor, chaos harness, SLO conformance, disaster recovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2770,7 +2770,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2.5","title":"[native-mux] Durable state + snapshot/checkpoint/rollback subsystem","description":"Implement durable control-plane state for sessions/fleets with checkpoint and rollback semantics that support failure recovery, migration safety, and auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Snapshot/checkpoint operations are atomic and recoverable. 2. Rollback semantics are explicit and tested with failure injection. 3. State schema supports orchestration and connector linkage.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:53.236278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:57.574295Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:57.574164Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-mux","persistence","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:53.236278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:34.481823Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1764,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"MistyLake: Implemented durable_state.rs module (commit e0733bea). Includes DurableStateManager with checkpoint/rollback, auto-checkpointing, state diffing, JSON serialization. 25+ inline tests, compiles cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:34:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2.6","title":"[native-mux] Headless/federated mux server for remote fleet control","description":"Deliver a production-grade headless mux server mode with remote control channels, enabling multi-host swarm operations and connector mesh adjacency without GUI coupling.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Headless server exposes secure remote control interfaces. 2. Multi-host operation model is validated. 3. Compatible with robot and orchestration layers.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:00.303699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:58.329498Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:58.329388Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-mux","remote","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:00.303699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:37.889350Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:42.942312Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:40.798465Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1766,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"headless_mux_server.rs implemented and committed (228ba05f). 967 lines: HeadlessMuxServer, RemoteRequest/Response protocol, federation management, peer health, checkpoint/rollback via remote. 20+ tests, 0 warnings. — MistyLake","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:54:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.2.7","title":"[quality] Native mux reliability/e2e validation harness","description":"Build dedicated validation harnesses for native mux invariants: lifecycle correctness, topology determinism, transport correctness, persistence integrity, and failover behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Harness covers happy-path, boundary, and failure-injection scenarios. 2. Deterministic replay proves mux invariants under concurrency and restart conditions. 3. Regression gates are enforceable in CI. 4. Includes deep unit/integration/e2e suites with structured logs and retained artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","notes":"PinkForge (2026-03-07): Added Section 12 (topology orchestration integration — 18 tests), Section 13 (cross-module: topology + durable state — 1 test), and Section 14 (cross-module: profile fleet + topology — 5 tests). Total harness now 60 tests (was 36). All pass. Coverage now includes: template registry, layout generation, plan validation, focus group lifecycle, audit logging, rebalance computation, serde roundtrips, cross-module checkpoint/rollback with topology changes, and profile-driven fleet → topology layout pipeline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonDune","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:24:01.211968Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T16:24:57.285246Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T16:24:57.285190Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","native-mux","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:24:01.211968Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:30.388318Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:31.484483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:32.313521Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:33.424891Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:34.758386Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:36.110675Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1767,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"20 cross-module e2e tests. 0 warnings. Commit 7bec15ab.","created_at":"2026-03-03T05:20:52Z"},{"id":1963,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"OliveHarbor: Added section 11 - policy trace propagation through command transport (6 tests). Total now 36 tests. All pass.","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:40:13Z"},{"id":1972,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge: Added sections 12-14 (24 new tests) + proptest_agent_pane_state.rs (35 tests). Total harness 60 tests, all pass.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:15:33Z"},{"id":1973,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.2.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge: Committed ebcb8be8 — sections 12-16 (35 new tests, 71 total) + proptest_agent_pane_state.rs (35 property tests). All green.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:24:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-3681t.3","title":"[epic] Native swarm orchestration runtime","description":"# Epic 3: Swarm Orchestration Runtime

Rebuild NTM's multi-agent orchestration semantics on FrankenTerm-native primitives: fleet launch, assignment, queueing, workflow execution, and automatic recovery.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Fleet launch, scheduling, and work distribution are native features.
2. Workflows/hooks/recovery execute reliably under contention.
3. Orchestration decisions are observable and policy-governed.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:42.531111Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:30:47.015136Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","orchestration","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:42.531111Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:48.535990Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1720,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design note: E3 captures the orchestration intelligence NTM proved valuable: launch semantics, adaptive scheduling, ready-work dispatch, and workflow automation. Difference here: this runtime is coupled to native mux and policy events instead of tmux command wrappers.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:55Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.3.1","title":"[swarm] Native fleet launcher with weighted agent orchestration","description":"Implement first-class fleet launch semantics: weighted model/program mix, role allocation, startup orchestration, and deterministic topology initialization backed by native mux profiles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_profiles.rs: FleetTemplate now supports program_mix_targets; FleetLaunchPlan now includes program_mix_deltas; added validation errors for empty/zero/duplicate program targets and tests (resolve_fleet_launch_order_deterministic_by_phase_weight_and_label + validate_program_mix_target_errors). Also patched compile fallout in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/native_mux_reliability_harness.rs to populate new FleetSlot/FleetTemplate fields. Additional compile-unblock patch in crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_work_queue.rs fixed would_create_cycle test argument types (&String vs &&str). Validation: rch exec cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests (remote failed-open to local) now finishes successfully with warnings only; targeted tests passed: session_profiles::tests::resolve_fleet_launch_order_deterministic_by_phase_weight_and_label and session_profiles::tests::validate_program_mix_target_errors. rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --tests -- -D warnings currently fails on pre-existing clippy::cargo_common_metadata in vendored packages (tabout/window), not this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:32:47Z"},{"id":1784,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"GrayHarbor taking a compile-unblock slice for this bead: reserved fleet/mission-mail surface and reproducing current failures via rch cargo check before patching. Scope: crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs + mission_agent_mail parity/tests.","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:55:50Z"},{"id":1797,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"Applied launch-mail contract hardening in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs: fleet launch start metadata now emits canonical snake_case startup strategy labels (parallel|sequential|phased) instead of Debug strings, plus unit assertion in emit_fleet_launch_start_notice_includes_fleet_metadata. Formatting check passed. Focused compile check succeeded via rch command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-grayharbor-slim rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime,agent-mail --message-format short (remote fail-opened local, build finished with 1 unrelated warning in pool.rs). Targeted mission_agent_mail test execution is currently blocked by unrelated syntax errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/export.rs (mismatched/unclosed delimiter around lines ~2649/~3122). Coordinating with export.rs lock holder (CyanGorge) for unblock.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:27:27Z"},{"id":1803,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GrayHarbor claiming support slice (non-overlap): mission Agent Mail launch metadata queryability hardening. Reserved crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs + crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mission_agent_mail_integration.rs. Planned changes: canonical strategy metadata assertions for start/progress notices; targeted rch validation and blocker reporting.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:33:13Z"},{"id":1804,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"BoldOtter support slice complete for `ft-3681t.3.1`: strengthened launch outcome queryability/determinism assertions in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs`.

Changes:
1. Added test `launch_outcome_registry_snapshot_is_queryable`:
   - exercises plan+execute path with mixed fleet
   - asserts `LaunchOutcome` summary fields (`status`, `total_slots`, `successful_slots`, `failed_slots`, `completed_at >= planned_at`)
   - verifies `registry_snapshot` is queryable and consistent:
     - session=1, window=1, panes=3, agents=3, total records=8
2. Hardened `execute_with_router_dispatches_bootstrap_commands`:
   - adds explicit profile fixture `agent-worker-bootstrap` with two bootstrap commands
   - launches two slots under that profile
   - asserts deterministic per-slot dispatch accounting: `[(0, 2), (1, 2)]`

Validation attempts:
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs` -> reports broad pre-existing formatting drift in this file (not introduced by this slice only).
- `timeout 240s rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core launch_outcome_registry_snapshot_is_queryable -- --nocapture`
  - remote worker selected + sync complete
  - remote exec failed (`Failed to connect to ubuntu@212.90.121.76`), rch failed-opened local
  - local run blocked on package cache lock under swarm concurrency; terminated to avoid violating strict offload preference

Residual blocker:
- Full targeted cargo proof for this slice remains blocked by unstable rch remote execution + local fallback lock contention.
","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:33:48Z"},{"id":1806,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GrayHarbor support slice complete (mission Agent Mail metadata queryability). Changes: (1) crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs now includes canonical startup strategy metadata on fleet launch progress notices (strategy = parallel|sequential|phased) in addition to start notices. (2) Added unit test emit_fleet_launch_progress_update_includes_strategy_metadata. (3) Strengthened crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mission_agent_mail_integration.rs to assert strategy=phased on both start and progress envelopes in fleet launch roundtrip. Formatting: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_agent_mail.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mission_agent_mail_integration.rs passed. Validation attempts (offload-only via rch): env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-grayharbor-mission-it2 timeout 120s rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mission_agent_mail_integration --no-default-features --features subprocess-bridge -- --nocapture -> remote exec SSH failure, fail-opened local compile, timed out (124) to avoid prolonged local execution; env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-grayharbor-mission-lib3 timeout 180s rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime,agent-mail --message-format short -> remote rsync failure, fail-opened local compile; interrupted to preserve offload policy.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:47:55Z"},{"id":1816,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GrayHarbor support slice complete (mission Agent Mail metadata queryability). Validation attempts (offload-only via rch):  -> remote exec SSH failure, fail-opened local compile, timed out (124) to avoid prolonged local execution;  -> remote rsync failure (missing remote transient files), fail-opened local compile; interrupted to preserve offload policy.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:52:08Z"},{"id":1817,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GrayHarbor starting follow-on support slice in fleet_launcher.rs: enforce explicit sequential startup failure semantics (mark downstream slots as Skipped after first sequential registration failure instead of continuing attempts) and add deterministic unit test coverage. Reservation active on crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:52:19Z"},{"id":1820,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GrayHarbor support slice landed in crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs: execute_with_subsystems now enforces explicit sequential failure semantics. In StartupStrategy::Sequential, after the first pane registration failure, remaining slots are marked SlotStatus::Skipped with reason text and are not provisioned. Added regression test execute_sequential_halts_after_first_failure_and_skips_remaining_slots that forces first-slot failure via pre-registration and verifies failed+skipped outcomes plus no additional pane registrations. Prior session work included in this bead scope:
- 20 property tests for ApprovalTracker workflow types (ee3c590b)
- 12 property tests for RevocationRegistry types (9769485b)
- Namespace isolation integration proptests (58b7b696)

I’ll keep the slice out of active dependency owners’ files where possible and avoid assuming blocked upstream features are complete.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:40:27Z"},{"id":1970,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"PlumGate","text":"Implemented a bounded precursor slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs: added UnifiedTelemetryRecord + source adapters for RuntimeTelemetryEvent, CanonicalConnectorEvent, and RecorderAuditEntry with normalized reason_code/correlation/scope/tier/failure/sensitivity fields, safe structural attribute projection, and focused unit tests. Local rustfmt on the touched file passes. Full cargo validation is currently blocked by RCH infrastructure: rch workers probe --all reports RCH-E100 SSH connection failures for all configured workers even though rch check/doctor report healthy. I invoked rch exec for a targeted cargo test, it failed open to local cargo, and I terminated the local fallback immediately to respect the repo rule that cargo work must be offloaded via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:57:49Z"},{"id":2021,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"GrayCitadel","text":"GrayCitadel taking a bounded follow-on slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs. Scope: thread UnifiedTelemetryRecord into incident/robot-facing runtime health enrichment with focused tests, while avoiding active policy/connector files. Agent Mail inbox rechecked and still empty; direct contact handshakes have been intermittently failing with MCP DB errors, so coordination is mirrored here as well.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:01:38Z"},{"id":2023,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"GrayCitadel","text":"GrayCitadel completed the bounded runtime_health follow-on slice for ft-3681t.7.1. Touched files: crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic_redaction.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_runtime_health.rs. Local file-level validation passed: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on all three touched files and git diff --check on the same paths. Compliant cargo validation remains blocked by RCH infrastructure in this environment, so I did not run or count any fail-open local cargo fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:04:05Z"},{"id":2087,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge: Added 35 property tests for policy_metrics module (2f345b93).  {"id":"ft-3681t.7.2","title":"[ops] Real-time dashboards and alerting for fleet health","description":"Deliver operational dashboards and alerting policies for swarm health, queue saturation, connector failures, policy denials, and recovery outcomes with actionable routing and runbook linkage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Dashboards expose actionable state (health, saturation, failures, policy denials, recoveries), not raw noise.
2. Alerts are tuned to reduce false positives/negatives and include severity-based routing/escalation.
3. Every alert class links to runbook guidance and captures operator ack/resolve trails.
4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e dashboard+alert tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:28.496447Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T20:01:18.187773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","dashboards","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:38:29.634935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:28.496447Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:26.175188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"closed_reason":"Fleet dashboard + alerting module: FleetAlertPolicy, FleetAlertManager, FleetDashboardView with 6 default policies and 26 tests.
